mnemonics: NODE-xt in a Chain: A Node has Data and a Link to the NEXT node.
keyPoints: A chain is a sequence of nodes linked together using pointers.
Each node is a struct containing data and a pointer to the next node.
The list ends with a NULL pointer, marking its termination.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to set the 'next' pointer of the last node to NULL, causing undefined behavior.
Accessing memory after freeing it (use-after-free) or not freeing allocated memory (memory leak).
